summary: Discovery stops at a nested governed root: skip descending any subdirectory (not the harness root) carrying its own `.temper/lock.toml` — its members are its own corpus, never the parent's.

edit.description: In `discoverable_paths`'s `filter_entry` (today excludes only `.git` and the `.temper` surface dir, TEMPER_DIR) also fence any directory below the harness root whose `.temper/lock.toml` exists, so `collect_glob`/the memory kind's `**/CLAUDE.md` root=`.` walk never descends a nested governed root. Exempt the harness root itself (its own `.temper/lock.toml` must not self-fence — key off DirEntry depth/path, not name).

tests.asserts: discover_kind_units over a harness containing a nested `.temper/lock.toml`-bearing subdir excludes that subdir's members; the harness root's own lock does not self-fence the walk
install::discover member counts exclude a nested governed root's CLAUDE.md — a vendored sub-harness adds no memory member to the parent

acceptance: The repo's own `temper check .temper` reports `memory (1)` — discovery no longer collects examples/base-harness/CLAUDE.md; a directory carrying its own `.temper/lock.toml` is fenced from the parent's walk (pipeline.md, Install).
notes: Live at HEAD: coverage.checked reports memory (2) — the repo's .temper/CLAUDE.md plus examples/base-harness/CLAUDE.md, wrongly collected by memory's `**/CLAUDE.md` root=`.` glob. The nested `.temper/` is already TEMPER_DIR-excluded; the fence is the parent-dir skip. Ruled John 07-15 ((discovery-nested-root-fence); dff2db2 body is the record). Vendored-harness inspection stays a future explicit verb ((vet)), never the ambient walk. scoped at dff2db2.

summary: Composed bodies interleave: blocks() accepts ordered `Text | EmbeddedMemberValue` children; emit renders prose spans and member fences in order, a narrative span staying prose (no wrapper member).

edit.description: Widen `Blocks.values` to `readonly (Text | EmbeddedMemberValue)[]` and `blocks(...)` to accept interleaved `Text | EmbeddedMemberValue` in authored order; a Text span carries its own mentions and includes. Update the doc comments (the `blocks()`/`Blocks` prose still says values-only).

In `renderBody`'s `blocks` branch (today `prose.values.map(renderMemberBlock)`), render each Text span via `renderText`/`resolveLeaf` and each EmbeddedMemberValue via `renderMemberBlock`, joined in authored order — the interleaved projection body.

A composed body's Text span contributes host-level `mention` rows (keyed `kind:name`, like a `text` body) and `include` rows, but NO nested-member row; only its EmbeddedMemberValue children yield nested-member + embedded-leaf-mention rows. Touches `mentionRows` (blocks branch, line ~338), `nestedMemberRows`, and the include-row derivation (retire the `blocks() body names none` include note at ~385).

tests.asserts: a composed body of interleaved text() spans and embedded member values renders in authored order; byte-identical to the same narrative carried by a wrapper (passage-style) member kind
a dangling mention inside a composed-body Text span refuses at emit like a member-level Text body; the span produces mention rows but no nested-member row

acceptance: blocks() with interleaved text() spans and member values compiles and emits an ordered body; a Text span's mentions/includes resolve (refusing on dangling) and produce no nested-member row; narrative no longer needs a wrapper kind to ride in a composed body (pipeline.md, The SDK).
notes: Ruled John 07-15 ((prose-interleaving); dff2db2 body is the record). Today blocks() takes EmbeddedMemberValue[] only, so base-harness mints a `passage` wrapper kind — the exhibit the ruling retires. SDK-only: payload body is a pre-rendered string, the mention/include/nested row families unchanged (no generated/ or Rust ripple). Follow-through (session docs(example), not this entry): base-harness drops `passage` for the native interleave, projected bytes unchanged. scoped at dff2db2.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Supersedes retired PACKAGING-CHANNELS (John, 07-11): both its file claims were dead — release.yml now exists (its `new` claim fails the refs gate) and the root-package.json launcher was ruled out (the launcher + optionalDependencies live on the SDK, distribution.md 'What ships'). Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. scoped at b676656.
